task_completion.description: Open the Electrical Engineering Calculations app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Electrical Engineering Calculations app interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select the DC resistance calculation tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the specific tool or section for calculating wire DC resistance.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct material, length, and area
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input fields show 'Copper' for material, '200' for length (meters), and '1.5' for cross-sectional area (mm²).
A calculation result is displayed on the screen, indicating the app has processed the inputs.

task_completion.description: Verify the calculated resistance is correct
task_completion.visual_evidence: The displayed resistance result is approximately 2.293 Ω (acceptable range is between 2.143 Ω and 2.443 Ω).

integrity.description: The agent used the app's graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Electrical Engineering Calculations app GUI, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The result is from the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches a genuine mobile application for electrical calculations, rather than a web browser search, a text editor, or a mocked-up image.

integrity.description: The result was computed by the app, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The result is shown in a read-only result field or label, not typed into an editable text input field by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected DC resistance result is 2.293 Ω (acceptable range: 2.143 Ω to 2.443 Ω). The required inputs are Copper material, 200m length, and 1.5 mm² cross-sectional area.
